title: FUGUEの実装を確認し、Kernelとの差分を埋めて。ただしFUGUE/kernel両方の既存のシステムを損壊しないように慎重に実装をマルチエージェントで進めて。
summary_text: 1. **High**: The compact artifact now always overwrites `context_reference` from `KERNEL_CONTEXT_REFERENCE_PATH/KIND/LABEL` whenever those env vars are set, with no existence validation. A stale/mistyped env value can silently persist a broken reference into artifacts and affect Kernel prompt/handoff context. [./scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh#L351), [./scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh#L361-L377), [./scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh#L663-L667) 1. **High**: `attach-context` remains an explicit/manual operator command in local flow; `run-local-orchestration.sh` only prints it (when `KERNEL_RUN_ID` exists) and does not execute it automatically. If users miss the step, continuation artifacts lose context-reference attachment unexpectedly, changing effective local handoff/runtime behavior versus prior implicit assumptions. [./scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh#L1113-L1120), [./scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh#L36-L37) 1. **Medium**: Local reference write failures are intentionally suppressed with `|| true`, so missing/invalid inputs can produce a “not attached” state without hard failure, which can hide regressions in local orchestration visibility. [./scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh#L1114-L1117) 1. **Medium**: `fugue-context-reference.sh` writes `issue_number` as a string and derives labels from JSON keys with minimal validation, so malformed references degrade into weak/unhelpful labels instead of failing fast or surfacing explicit errors. [./scripts/local/fugue-context-reference.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/fugue-context-reference.sh#L25-L31), [./scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh#L653-L661) 1. **Low**: Handoff routing now switches to `scripts/harness/kernel-handoff.sh` when present for kernel target, which alters execution path even when previous direct workflow dispatch was used. This is a behavior shift even if outputs are equivalent, so any divergence in adapter expectations could affect orchestration. [./scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L540-L606), [./scripts/harness/kernel-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/kernel-handoff.sh#L1-L206)
